statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Git commit messages. A commit message is valid if it consists of a title (maximum of 50 characters) followed by a newline character and then a body. The title should not be empty or contain more than one newline character, and the body should not be empty.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git commit message
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the commit message is valid
statement.notes: The commit message may contain multiple newline characters in the title or body, but there should be exactly one newline character separating the title and the body.

constraints.description: The maximum length of the title and body

examples.input:
Fix bug

This is a commit message
examples.explanation: The title is 'Fix bug' and the body is 'This is a commit message'. The commit message is valid.

examples.input:
This is a title

This is a commit message
examples.explanation: The title contains more than one newline character, so the commit message is invalid.
The title is empty, so the commit message is invalid.

hints.text: Check if the title is not empty and contains at most one newline character

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Git Commit Message Validation
editorial.intuition: To validate a Git commit message, we need to check if the title is not empty and contains at most one newline character, and if the body is not empty.
editorial.bruteForce: We can iterate over the characters in the commit message to check if the title meets the requirements.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use string splitting to separate the title and the body, and then check the length and newline characters in the title.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can split the commit message into two parts at the first newline character, and then check the length and newline characters in the first part.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The commit message is valid if and only if the title is not empty, contains at most one newline character, and the body is not empty.
editorial.dryRun:
Let's take the commit message 'Fix bug

This is a commit message' as an example. We can split it into two parts at the first newline character to get 'Fix bug' and 'This is a commit message'. The title 'Fix bug' meets the requirements, so the commit message is valid.
commonMistakes: forgetting to handle the case where the title is empty
forgetting to handle the case where the title contains more than one newline character

testCases.input:
Fix bug

This is a commit message

This is a title

This is a commit message
